layman_headline: Extreme Fear grips the market — patience beats FOMO right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 25/100, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.9%, which means money is flowing away from altcoins and into Bitcoin. The swing candidates available today are mostly low-quality setups: many have already rallied 20-35% in the past week (making them extended and risky to chase), while others are in clear downtrends with bearish chart structures. There's no clean entry with a strong risk/reward ratio right now.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait for a cleaner setup. The best swing trades come when fear peaks and a coin shows clear technical reversal signals — we're not quite there yet on any candidate with sufficient quality.
expert_reasoning: Systematically evaluating all 9 candidates: DEXE has conflicting signals (price data shows $9.98 but chart says $36.27, RSI 71 overbought despite a -75% drop — data integrity issues make this untradeable). 币安人生 has a bullish chart (bull flag, +0.80 bias) but risk/reward is only 0.97x and RSI at 58 after a -24% drop suggests more selling ahead. UB shows a Morning Star with 3.44x R/R but is already +35% in 7 days — extended. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85, Three White Soldiers) but terrible R/R of 0.38x and is +20% in 7 days. ONDO is similarly extended at +28%. ETH has approaching-oversold RSI (39) which is a reliable signal per our track record (83% win rate), but the chart bias is bearish (-0.70) and price is below all MAs — no reversal confirmation yet. NIGHT is in a clear downtrend. VVV is slightly bearish. MON lacks any technical data. With BTC dominance at 56.9%, altcoin setups carry extra headwind risk. The disciplined move is to wait for ETH to either confirm a reversal (reclaim SMA20, RSI dip to 30-35) or for a fresh catalyst-driven setup.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.9% creates persistent headwinds for all altcoin positions
Extreme Fear at 25 can persist or deepen — catching a falling knife is the primary risk
Most candidates have already moved significantly, leaving poor risk/reward entries
watch_list: ETH RSI approaching 30 with a bullish reversal candle — would trigger a high-conviction buy
BTC dominance trend — a rollover below 56% would signal altcoin rotation beginning
Fear & Greed index — a spike below 20 followed by recovery would be a classic capitulation signal
market_context: The crypto market is in Extreme Fear (25/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.9%, indicating a risk-off environment where capital favors Bitcoin over altcoins. BTC itself is showing strength (+4% weekly) supported by institutional inflows, but this hasn't translated into broad altcoin recovery yet.
context_banner: High BTC dominance and Extreme Fear create a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades — discipline demands waiting for confirmed reversal setups rather than chasing extended bounces.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25/100 with flat trend suggests capitulation hasn't completed — buying too early in fear carries significant drawdown risk.
ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold (our most reliable signal at 83% win rate) but hasn't reached the 30 threshold for a high-conviction entry.
LDO and UB show bullish chart patterns (+0.85 and +0.80 bias) but both have already rallied 20-35% making entries risky with poor R/R ratios.
Bitcoin rally has broad-based institutional support with whales and options traders piling in — bullish for BTC but high dominance means alts are starved of capital.
ETH derivatives reset noted by Blockworks suggests positioning is being unwound — could set up a cleaner entry in coming days once the reset completes.
